title: JD Fit Decoder — Extraction Output Schema
description: The exact structure the LLM must return when parsing a job description. Designed to be auditable: every extracted requirement must carry the source text it came from, so a human can verify the LLM didn't invent or misread something.

requirements.description: Every distinct requirement found in the JD, atomized to one skill/qualification per entry. Do not bundle multiple skills into one entry.

text.description: Normalized, concise statement of the requirement (e.g. 'SQL proficiency', not the full sentence it came from). This is the human-readable label shown in the UI.

canonical_skill.description: A short, canonical name for this skill/tool/domain/certification, suitable for exact-string matching against a profile (e.g. text='SQL proficiency' -> canonical_skill='SQL'; text='Running experiments and A/B testing' -> canonical_skill='A/B testing'). Use the most standard, widely-recognized name for the underlying skill — not a paraphrase of the JD's specific wording. If a requirement doesn't correspond to a single matchable skill/tool/domain/cert (skill_type is 'years_experience', 'seniority', or 'other'), repeat the text value here.

category.description: must_have = stated as required/essential, or implied by strong language ('strong experience in', 'proven track record'). nice_to_have = explicitly optional, bonus, or 'preferred'. If genuinely ambiguous, default to nice_to_have and lower the confidence score rather than guessing must_have — the cost of under-counting a requirement is lower than the cost of penalizing a candidate for something that wasn't really required.

skill_type.description: What kind of requirement this is — lets the scorer match it against the right part of the profile (skills vs tools vs domains, etc.).

minimum_years.description: If the JD states a minimum years requirement for this item (e.g. '5+ years SQL'), extract the number. Null if not stated.

confidence.description: LLM's confidence that this requirement was correctly identified and categorized. Below 0.6 should be flagged for human review.

source_quote.description: The exact verbatim sentence or phrase from the JD this requirement was extracted from. Mandatory — used for auditability and to catch hallucinated requirements.

seniority_signal.description: Overall seniority level the JD appears to be targeting, independent of any single requirement.

reasoning.description: One sentence explaining what in the JD signals this level (e.g. job title, years required, scope of ownership described).

domain_signals.description: Industry/domain context mentioned or implied (e.g. 'EdTech', 'FinTech') — separate from requirements list because domain fit is scored differently from skill fit.

required.description: True if the JD explicitly requires this domain background, false if it's just contextual (e.g. company description) and not a stated requirement.

flags.description: Notable patterns worth surfacing to the user that aren't simple requirements — vague scope, contradictory signals, unrealistic combinations, location/visa mentions, etc.
